macro_summary: SPY climbed 0.76% to $679.91 and QQQ gained 0.94% to $610.19 as markets absorbed another volatile session in the Iran-Hormuz saga now entering its 32nd day. VIX fell sharply by 7.37% to 19.49, its lowest reading in weeks, as news reports of renewed de-escalation efforts between Israel and Lebanon provided a secondary relief catalyst — per Yahoo Finance, 'a number of stocks jumped in the afternoon session after investors reacted to the news that Israel and Lebanon might enter direct negotiations, further supporting the fragile US-Iran ceasefire.' The March CPI report is due this morning at 08:30 ET and is the single most important catalyst for rate expectations this week; per US Bank Asset Management commentary, the Fed held rates at 3.50-3.75% in March and signaled it wants 'more confidence that inflation is moving down in a lasting way' before cutting. SOXX surged 3.03% to $378.63, its strongest single-session performance in over two weeks, led by INTC (+5.96%) on the TeraFab partnership announcement and AMD (+3.02%) on momentum flows.
commodities_note: WTI settled at $98.37, down 0.37% on the session, but the headline number masks significant intraday volatility — the Hormuz closure remains in effect and per Euronews, OPEC+'s planned 206,000 bpd output hike for May 'represents less than 2% of the supply disrupted by the Hormuz closure,' making it largely symbolic. J.P. Morgan noted in a Thursday note that oil could reach $150/bbl if supply disruption persists to mid-May. Gold eased 0.21% to $4,783/oz — still historically elevated but pulling back from recent highs; no attractive entry exists in GLD given the extended run. The DXY was essentially flat at 98.91 (-0.01%), reflecting a market in wait-and-see mode ahead of CPI. US10Y ticked up modestly to 4.29%, holding above the 4.20% threshold that matters for Singapore bank NIM compression.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

D05_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. DBS is 1.4% above its 50-day average of SGD 56.49, with RSI at 60.7 — technically on the edge of the acceptable entry zone but nudging toward overbought territory. The tight setup here is notable: nearest resistance sits at SGD 57.47 (just 0.3% above price), which the pipeline flags as inside a single session's normal range; the meaningful ceiling is SGD 58.78 (2.6% above). Invalidation is the 50-day average at SGD 56.49 — a break there confirms the rally has failed, but at only 1.4% below current price, normal intraday volatility could trigger this. Use the structural support at SGD 52.36 (8.6% below) as the mental stop for position sizing. The R:R of 1.9:1 to SGD 58.78 is just below the 2.0 threshold for ACCUMULATE eligibility. NIM compression persists structurally — US10Y remains above the 4.20% reset threshold at 4.29%, and analyst consensus expects earnings to decline 8.8% forward. The 5.35% dividend yield provides meaningful support for patience, and at 13.9x forward PE, DBS trades at a modest 10% premium to the Singapore cluster median — not stretched given its franchise quality. Volume is below average, which argues against conviction entry. Bottom line: DBS offers a solid yield at a reasonable price, but the entry zone is tight and NIM headwinds haven't resolved. Wait for either a pullback to SGD 56.50-57.00 or a confirmed break of US10Y below 4.20% before entering.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

O39_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to CAUTION — RSI hit 65.6, triggering the hard block at >65, and the pipeline has enforced a hard entry block. OCBC has surged 4.88% above its 50-day average of SGD 21.41 in just 13 sessions on a rising SMA, and RSI at 65.6 is at the doorstep of overbought — the risk/reward of chasing here is poor with a derived R:R of just 1.1:1 to resistance at SGD 23.57. The structural support at SGD 18.61 (17.1% below) is the floor that matters if momentum reverses. Of the Singapore banks, OCBC is the standout — it is the only bank with YoY profit growth and its forward PE of 12.6x is exactly in line with the cluster median, while the 3.79% yield remains competitive. But the entry timing is wrong: buying into a 7.6% one-month surge at RSI 66 with below-average volume is momentum-chasing, not disciplined entry. Bottom line: OCBC is the best-quality Singapore bank on the watchlist, but this isn't the right price to enter — wait for a pullback toward the 50-day average around SGD 21.40-21.80 before considering a position.

U11_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. UOB is sitting precisely on its 50-day average of SGD 37.33 (0.17% below) with RSI at 56.2 — technically neutral in every dimension. The tight_upside flag applies: nearest resistance is SGD 37.40 (0.4% above, inside a single day's move), so the meaningful ceiling is SGD 39.49 (6.0% above), giving a R:R of 1.4:1 to the 200-day SMA invalidation at SGD 35.68 — below the 2.0 threshold for ACCUMULATE. Analyst consensus expects earnings to decline 6.7%, and with revenue contracting 1.8% and a PEG of 2.02, the valuation lacks a compelling growth story. The 10.2x forward PE represents a 19% discount to the Singapore cluster median, which is value territory — but that discount reflects the weaker fundamentals, not an overlooked opportunity. NIM compression headwinds remain intact with US10Y above 4.20%. Bottom line: UOB is cheap for a reason — upgrades to WATCH if price holds above SGD 37.33 with volume, or on a confirmed US10Y break below 4.20%.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

NVDA.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. NVDA reclaimed its 50-day average of $182.13 for the first time in over a month — but only one session above it, and volume was below average, which argues against conviction. The tight setup bears flagging: nearest resistance is $184.47 (0.3% above, inside a day's move); the meaningful target is $194.67 (5.9% above), giving an observed R:R of 5.9:1 — highly favourable on paper, but the tight invalidation at $182.13 (just 1.0% below) means normal volatility could trigger the stop before the thesis is tested. Use the structural support at $180.52 (1.8% below) as the mental stop for position sizing. The Amazon annual letter flagged Andy Jassy's aspiration to sell AI chips externally — per Yahoo Finance, this 'could end up challenging Nvidia' — but this is a years-out strategic posture, not a near-term revenue threat to NVDA's Blackwell Ultra ramp guiding $78B in Q1 FY2027. At 16.5x forward PE with a 0.72 PEG and 73% revenue growth, NVDA trades at a 21% discount to the Semis cluster median — genuinely cheap for the quality. The FCF yield of 1.3% is thin, meaning the thesis depends on continued earnings execution. Fragility gate check: the thesis rests primarily on the Blackwell ramp; a second leg (hyperscaler capex confirmation in Q1 earnings) would be needed to upgrade to BUY. NVDA reports May 21 — no near-term binary event. Bottom line: NVDA is the cheapest high-quality name in the Semis cluster relative to its growth rate. Wait for a second consecutive close above $182 with above-average volume before entering — one session does not confirm the reclaim.

AMD.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. AMD has surged 12.95% above its 50-day average of $209.50 over just five sessions — the pipeline hard-blocks entry at this extension level, and rightly so: RSI at 66.8, SMA50 not yet rising, and only 5 sessions above the average versus the 30-session minimum required for a trend exception. The Meta-AMD GPU framework agreement (per AMD IR, February 24, 2026) provides genuine long-term credibility as a hyperscaler-scale NVDA alternative, but the key distinction holds — this is a framework ceiling of up to 6GW, not a firm purchase order; first shipments are H2 2026. Aletheia Capital reiterated Buy with a $333 target. At 21.9x forward PE with a 0.65 PEG and 34.1% revenue growth, AMD is the best-valued name in the Semis cluster on a growth-adjusted basis — but the R:R from here is 1.1:1 to $265.85 versus a 11.5% downside to SMA50 invalidation, which is simply unfavourable. AMD earnings are May 6 — 26 days out. Bottom line: AMD is the most fundamentally compelling setup on the watchlist but the entry price is wrong right now — wait for a pullback to the $215-225 zone before considering entry.

INTC.entry_block: BLOCKED: +31.5% above 50-day SMA (>20% extreme extension). No trend exception applies at this level — entry requires a meaningful pullback regardless of trend duration.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

INTC.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD — but the developments today are significant. Intel is 31.5% above its 50-day average of $46.94, triggering the extreme extension hard block; RSI is at 74.8 (overbought) with volume above average. Two major catalysts emerged: per Benzinga (citing Counterpoint Research analyst Neil Shah), Intel, Tesla, xAI, and SpaceX finalized a landmark TeraFab partnership positioning Intel as a core technology and manufacturing partner for next-generation AI and semiconductor production — a credibility boost for the foundry arm. Separately, per Simply Wall St., Intel and Google agreed a multi-year collaboration to co-develop custom infrastructure processing units (IPUs) for AI and cloud workloads. These are meaningful foundry validation events, but the 18A thesis qualifier holds: no confirmed external customer has publicly committed to 18A volume production — the TeraFab and Google partnerships are manufacturing and IP partnerships, not confirmed 18A wafer commitments. The current R:R is 0.2:1 (5.0% upside to $64.81 versus 23.9% downside to SMA50 invalidation) — objectively unfavourable. Intel reports April 24 (14 days out). Forward PE of 61.5x versus the Semis median of 21x (193% premium), negative FCF yield of -1.45%, and negative revenue growth of -4.1% make the valuation dependent entirely on foundry execution materializing. Bottom line: The TeraFab and Google wins are real progress — but at 31% above its 50-day average and RSI at 75, the entry is terrible. Wait for a pullback toward $52-55 before evaluating seriously.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

ASML.signal_rationale: Signal changed from BUY to WATCH — ASML's R:R has deteriorated to 0.8:1 (3.0% upside to $1,491.51 versus 4.0% downside to the $1,391.16 SMA50 invalidation), which falls below the 1.0 minimum threshold for a BUY signal. Price is 4.13% above the 50-day average — technically in the 2-5% 'slightly extended' zone that would require a catalyst override, but the R:R no longer supports it. Volume was normal rather than confirming. ASML earnings are April 15 — five trading days away — which introduces binary event risk that argues against initiating a position this week. The MATCH Act, as reported by Simply Wall St., would tighten export controls on ASML's DUV (deep ultraviolet) equipment sales and servicing to China, adding a new regulatory headwind. ASML's PEG of 2.18 and forward PE of 33.1x (57.6% premium to the Semis median) reflect a premium valuation that requires clean execution. The TSM-ASML linkage remains intact: TSM earnings are April 16 (six days out), and strong CoWoS demand confirmation would be a positive read-through for ASML order flow. Bottom line: ASML is a high-quality name at a fair-to-premium price, but with earnings in five days and R:R unfavourable at current levels, patience is warranted — revisit after the April 15 earnings print.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

MU.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. MU surged 14.59% over the past five sessions — the pipeline hard-blocks entry on momentum-chase grounds (>10% five-day move), and the current R:R of 0.9:1 (3.8% upside to $437.65 versus 4.4% downside to the $403.11 SMA50 invalidation) is unfavourable. The helium supply angle is real: per the Wall Street Journal, 'Investors have watched for the Middle East cease-fire to unlock supplies of helium, which is key to chip production' — Hormuz disruption affects helium export routes, and any credible reopening would reduce a genuine input cost for semiconductor fabrication. BofA Securities reiterated support, noting per Insider Monkey that 'the global push for AI infrastructure is moving into a more stable phase.' The valuation metrics are anomalous: forward PE of 4.3x versus trailing PE of 19.9x implies a massive earnings acceleration priced in (756% analyst consensus earnings growth), while the stated PEG of 0.21 with a forward PE above 4x is internally inconsistent — per the valuation sanity rule, verify earnings growth estimates before citing PEG as a primary argument. FCF yield of 0.61% is thin. The inference-driven HBM memory demand thesis remains intact; any CEO guidance on memory prices carries inherent seller bias. Bottom line: MU has the strongest near-term earnings growth setup in the Semis cluster, but after a 14.6% five-day surge, chasing here is high risk — wait for a consolidation back toward $403-415 before entering.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

TSM.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. TSM is 4.62% above its rising 50-day average of $349.35 — in the slightly extended zone, just below the 5% hard block threshold, with RSI at 60.7 and volume below average. The R:R is 0.8:1 (3.7% upside to $378.93 versus 4.4% downside to SMA50 invalidation) — technically unfavourable for entry at current levels. Importantly, TSM earnings are April 16 — six trading days away — making this the most time-sensitive name on the watchlist. The earnings print will provide critical read-through on CoWoS packaging capacity (currently scaling toward 120-130K wafers/month by end-2026), NVDA Blackwell Ultra ramp utilization, and whether TSM's 5-10% ASP hikes on sub-5nm nodes are flowing through to margins. Per IBD, Trump issued a Hormuz threat before Iran peace talks Thursday evening — any escalation re-pricing would disrupt TSM's supply chain indirectly via helium and specialty gas routes. At 20.2x forward PE with a 1.21 PEG and 20.5% revenue growth, TSM is fairly valued versus the Semis median — not cheap, not stretched. Bottom line: TSM is the kingmaker of the AI buildout and the cleanest supply-chain name on the watchlist, but entering six days before a binary earnings event with below-average volume and unfavourable near-term R:R is poor timing — revisit after the April 16 print.

AMZN.entry_block: BLOCKED: +9.4% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=2 (<30), sma50 not rising, RSI 69 (>=55).

AMZN.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to CAUTION — AMZN surged 6.05% on the session and 10.96% over five days, pushing 9.44% above its 50-day average of $213.50. The pipeline hard-blocks entry at this extension level, and the tight_upside flag applies: nearest resistance is $235.31 (0.7% above, inside a day's move); the meaningful ceiling is $238.91. The current R:R is 0.3:1 (2.3% upside versus 8.6% downside to SMA50 invalidation) — decisively unfavourable. The catalyst driving today's surge is CEO Andy Jassy's annual shareholder letter, in which he highlighted AWS AI business strength and raised the possibility of selling AI chips externally — per Yahoo Finance, this 'raised stakes for Nvidia, AMD.' The chip-selling aspiration is a multi-year strategic direction, not a near-term revenue item. The $200B AI capex commitment (per Q4 2025 10-K filing) continues to weigh on near-term operating income visibility. At 24.9x forward PE with a 1.61 PEG and only 5% expected earnings growth (with 13.6% revenue growth), the growth-to-multiple ratio is mediocre for the valuation. AMZN reports May 1. Structural support at $197.66 (15.4% below) is the floor that matters if momentum reverses. Bottom line: AMZN's AWS AI story is genuinely exciting but the stock just ran 11% in five days — no entry exists at current levels. Avoid until a pullback to the $213-221 zone.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

GOOG.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. Alphabet is 2.67% above its 50-day average of $308.15, placing it in the slightly extended but not hard-blocked zone, with RSI at 62.7 and volume below average. The tight_upside flag applies: nearest resistance is $319.23 (0.9% above, inside normal daily range); the meaningful R:R uses $328.22 as the target — giving 1.4:1 (3.7% upside versus 2.6% downside to SMA50 invalidation). That's below the 2.0 threshold for ACCUMULATE. The Intel-Google multi-year collaboration on custom IPUs (per Simply Wall St. and GuruFocus) is a supply-chain diversification move — reducing Google's reliance on NVDA GPUs for cloud workloads — but the primary thesis for GOOG remains the TPU v7 dominance story and GCP growth. The TurboQuant algorithm displacement narrative remains a latent overhang: Q1 earnings (April 30) will be the first opportunity to assess whether GPU demand guidance holds or weakens. The forward PE of 23.6x is slightly below the BigTech cluster median of 24.2x, and the PEG of 2.21 is slightly above 2.0, flagging a mild valuation concern — revenue growth of 18% versus a 23.6x multiple is a modest mismatch. FCF yield of 1.0% is thin. Bottom line: Google is fairly priced for its growth rate, but the entry setup is slightly extended and the R:R is subpar — wait for a pullback to $308-312 before entering.

MSFT.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to WATCH — MSFT has pulled back 5.75% below its 50-day average of $395.82 and RSI has declined to 40.0, approaching oversold territory, which represents a meaningful improvement in entry conditions. The R:R is 6.3:1 (30.8% upside to the $488.02 resistance versus 4.9% downside to the $354.62 support invalidation) — highly favourable by ratio, but the upside target is a distant resistance zone, not a near-term level. All 8 ACCUMULATE gates are checked: caution_source was base_scorer ✓, R:R ratio 6.3 ✓, rr_quality 'observed' ✓, but the R:R uses a very distant upside target ($488.02), which makes the ratio mechanically large without reflecting near-term reality — the nearest meaningful resistance is likely in the $395-400 zone (the declining 50-day average), implying a realistic near-term R:R closer to 1:1. ACCUMULATE is not assigned. At 19.8x forward PE with 59.8% expected earnings growth and a 1.22 PEG, MSFT's valuation has become genuinely attractive after the 8% one-month decline. However, the OpenAI concentration risk is the critical qualifier: approximately 45% of MSFT's $625B commercial revenue backlog is tied to a single customer, and Azure AI growth includes circular flows from MSFT's own $13B+ OpenAI investment returning as Azure consumption credits. Non-OpenAI enterprise RPO (~$350B growing 28%) is the cleaner signal to watch. MSFT reports April 30. Baird tech strategist Ted Mortonson, per Yahoo Finance, called out MSFT as having 'what it takes to survive the AI disruption trade.' Bottom line: Microsoft has gotten genuinely cheap relative to its earnings power — the entry setup is improving. Wait for RSI to stabilize above 42 with a volume uptick before entering, or set a limit order at the $354-360 support zone.

LITE.sma50_warning: SMA50 is 27% below price — pullback to SMA50 would require a crash-level move. Use shorter-term support levels for entry guidance instead.
LITE.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. LITE is 37.85% above its 50-day average of $648.63 — extreme extension with a hard block at this level, no exceptions. The SMA50 warning is explicit: at 27% below price, a pullback to the 50-day average would be a crash-level move; use the shorter-term support zones at $642.37-$573.73 instead. FCF yield is negative at -0.03%, and the PEG of 0.16 with a forward PE of 56.7x is flagged as anomalous — verify earnings growth estimates before citing PEG as a valuation argument. The PEG appears to reflect an extreme forward earnings ramp that may be over-extrapolated. The NVDA $2B strategic investment (confirmed via NVIDIA investor relations, March 2, 2026) and S&P 500 inclusion (March 9, 2026) are the structural drivers; S&P inclusion buying typically exhausts within 3-5 sessions of the effective date, per standing heuristics — that window has long passed. BNP Paribas raised its price target to $1,040 (per Insider Monkey). The Coherent debt-for-equity exchange of ~$475M in convertible notes for ~5.7M shares (per Simply Wall St.) improves the balance sheet. None of this changes the entry calculus at 37.9% extension. Bottom line: LITE is the right company in the right place — optical interconnects are the AI datacenter bandwidth bottleneck — but the price is wrong by a wide margin. Avoid until a meaningful pullback to the $700-750 zone.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

COHR.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Coherent is 15.72% above its 50-day average of $245.56 — hard-blocked from entry, but notably different from LITE in that the SMA50 is rising and the pipeline assigns HOLD rather than CAUTION. The R:R is 0.4:1 (5.6% upside to $300.20 resistance versus 13.6% downside to SMA50 invalidation) — unfavourable for entry here. The 6-inch indium phosphide wafer ramp to double output by year-end (per Simply Wall St.) is a concrete operational deliverable for the NVDA AI optics partnership (confirmed via NVIDIA newsroom, March 2, 2026). Stifel raised its price target to $275 (per Insider Monkey) but 24/7 Wall St. has a $201.63 sell target, illustrating the wide analyst dispersion. The PEG of 0.06 with a 38.3x forward PE is flagged as anomalous — the denominator implies extraordinary earnings growth expectations that warrant independent verification. FCF yield of 0.24% is negligible, meaning the thesis is entirely growth-dependent. COHR reports May 7. Bottom line: Coherent is executing on its AI optics ramp and the NVDA partnership is real, but the entry price is wrong — upgrades to WATCH on a pullback to the $250-260 zone with volume confirmation.

PLTR.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 70x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
PLTR.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. PLTR fell 7.54% on high volume — the largest single-session decline on the watchlist — after Michael Burry publicly stated (per Yahoo Finance) that 'Anthropic is eating Palantir's lunch,' citing Anthropic's $30B revenue trajectory as evidence that 'brains' (foundation model providers) will displace 'infrastructure' (data analytics platforms like PLTR). The post is reportedly now deleted but the damage to sentiment is done. PLTR sits 9.96% below its 50-day average of $144.92 with RSI at 35.6 — approaching but not yet at the RSI <38 contrarian threshold. The R:R of 0.9:1 (3.0% upside to the $134.39 resistance versus 3.2% downside to the $126.30 support invalidation) is unfavourable. The forward PE of 70.1x (189.7% premium to the BigTech cluster median of 24.2x) remains the fundamental blocker — the Anthropic threat, even if overstated, adds a second layer of valuation skepticism to a stock already priced for perfection. The $10B Army IDIQ framework (a ceiling, not firm revenue) and $448M Navy ShipOS deal (per DoD press release) remain intact. Defense AI budget cycle remains favorable. RSI at 35.6 is 2.4 points above the 33.0 threshold that would make PLTR a contrarian candidate — monitor for further deterioration. Bottom line: PLTR's defense AI franchise is real, but at 70x forward earnings with a public thesis challenge from a credible investor, the entry risk is asymmetric — avoid until RSI confirms oversold or the stock reclaims $134-135 on volume.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

WRD.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. WeRide is 4.69% above its 50-day average of $7.25 with RSI at 54.1 — the entry is slightly extended but within the 5% threshold, and the R:R of 2.0:1 (9.2% upside to $8.29 versus 4.5% downside to SMA50 invalidation at $7.25) exactly meets the ACCUMULATE minimum threshold. However, ACCUMULATE requires rr_quality of 'observed' ✓, ratio ≥2.0 ✓, but the ADR/China regulatory risk is a standing invalidator that constitutes a specific thesis-level concern — US-China delisting legislation or Chinese data-export crackdowns on autonomous vehicles would immediately break the thesis, and this risk is elevated in the current tariff environment. The Singapore Punggol launch with Grab (per Simply Wall St. and Insider Monkey, April 1, 2026) — free weekday autonomous shuttle rides before a planned mid-2026 commercial rollout — is a concrete commercial milestone: WeRide and Grab have logged 30,000km of testing and begun retraining driver-partners as Safety Operators. This validates the international commercialization thesis ('first L4 company to commercialize internationally at scale'). Revenue growth of 123% YoY is exceptional; the path to profitability (operating loss narrowing only 15.5% on 90% revenue growth) requires monitoring. WRD reports May 21. Bottom line: WeRide is commercializing faster than any L4 peer internationally, and the Grab-Singapore launch is a meaningful proof point — but the ADR regulatory overhang and pre-profit financials justify a cautious starter entry only on a pullback to $7.25-7.40.

singapore.summary: The Singapore bank cluster remains structurally constrained — US10Y at 4.29% holds above the 4.20% NIM reset threshold for a 30th consecutive session, keeping earnings compression intact for DBS and UOB. OCBC's relative outperformance (7.6% one-month gain versus DBS at 4.3% and UOB at 3.2%) reflects its status as the only bank with YoY profit growth, but the RSI hard block at 65.6 now moves OCBC to CAUTION — the divergence within the cluster is flipping from 'OCBC is a buy' to 'OCBC has run too far.' MAS Monetary Policy Statement on April 14 is the most important near-term catalyst for the cluster.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally constrained — NIM compression ongoing, no reset catalyst yet
singapore.key_development: OCBC RSI breach triggers hard block, moving the cluster's best-performing name to CAUTION just as MAS policy statement approaches April 14.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX surged 3.03% to $378.63, its strongest session in weeks, led by INTC's TeraFab and Google partnership news rather than broad-based buying — individual SOXX components tell a bifurcated story. ASML flipped from BUY to WATCH as its R:R deteriorated with price extension, and both ASML (April 15) and TSM (April 16) face binary earnings events within a week that make new entries inadvisable. The 'mid-cap foundry resilience vs. mega-cap duration drag' rotation thesis is being tested: AMD is 13% above its SMA50 after a 12.6% five-day run, while NVDA has barely reclaimed its SMA50 — the divergence is unusual given AMD's greater extension risk.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Bifurcated — INTC/AMD extended, NVDA/TSM/ASML approaching key earnings events
semiconductors.key_development: INTC's TeraFab partnership with Tesla, xAI, and SpaceX (per Benzinga/Counterpoint Research) provides the most credible foundry validation signal in months, but at 31% above SMA50, the stock cannot be entered.

big_tech.summary: The BigTech cluster saw dramatic divergence: AMZN surged 6.05% on Jassy's shareholder letter AWS commentary to 9.44% above its SMA50 (hard block), PLTR fell 7.54% on the Burry-Anthropic thesis challenge, MSFT slipped 0.25% further below its declining SMA50, and GOOG drifted +0.53%. The cluster has now split into two groups: AMZN/GOOG recovering on AI capex conviction, and MSFT/PLTR under structural pressure. The TurboQuant overhang persists across all four names — Q1 earnings season (GOOG and MSFT both reporting April 30) will be the first real data point on whether capex guidance holds.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — AMZN extended, PLTR thesis challenged, MSFT improving setup
big_tech.key_development: Amazon CEO Jassy's chip-selling aspiration in the annual letter creates a new long-term competitive dynamic for NVDA and AMD, though the timeline for external chip sales remains speculative.

photonics.summary: Both LITE and COHR remain hard-blocked from entry due to extreme extension (LITE at 37.9% above SMA50, COHR at 15.7%), with LITE's negative FCF yield and anomalous PEG metrics adding fundamental caution. The NVDA $2B investment in each (confirmed via NVIDIA IR, March 2, 2026) remains the thesis anchor, but S&P 500 inclusion buying exhausted weeks ago — current price levels reflect structural AI optics demand premium, not inclusion flows. The cluster divergence (LITE as the high-beta play, COHR as the more operationally grounded name) continues.
photonics.thesis_status: Thesis intact, entry blocked — structural AI optics demand confirmed but extreme extension
photonics.key_development: Coherent's 6-inch indium phosphide wafer ramp to double output by year-end (per Simply Wall St.) is the most concrete near-term operational milestone in the cluster.

energy.summary: No energy names (XLE) remain on the active watchlist following the signal transitions of recent sessions, but WTI at $98.37 and the Hormuz crisis entering week 32 keep the energy macro backdrop highly relevant. OPEC+'s 206,000 bpd May hike (per Euronews) is symbolic — less than 2% of disrupted supply — and J.P. Morgan's $150/bbl upside scenario if disruption persists to mid-May remains a live tail risk.
energy.thesis_status: Watching from sidelines — no energy names at actionable entry levels
energy.key_development: Trump's Tuesday deadline for Iran to open Hormuz (per IBD) is the most time-sensitive macro event of the week, with direct implications for oil price direction.

index.summary: SPY at $679.91 and QQQ at $610.19 extended their relief rally for a second session, with VIX dropping to 19.49 — a notable improvement from last week's 30+ readings. The S&P 500 technical picture has stabilized above the 100-day and 50-day averages, but conviction depends entirely on tonight's CPI print and whether the Iran peace talks produce durable de-escalation. The Russell 2000 continues to outperform Nasdaq per the tariff rotation narrative (Congress.gov overview), reflecting investors' continued flight from multinationals.
index.thesis_status: Cautiously recovering — VIX normalization supportive but geopolitical and inflation risks remain
index.key_development: VIX falling to 19.49 (-7.37%) removes the most acute volatility premium from index pricing, but this is the third VIX single-day drop above 7% in six weeks — per standing heuristics, confirm with breadth before treating as trend reversal.

interconnected.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (ADR)
interconnected.reason: TSM earnings April 16 will provide the definitive read-through on CoWoS packaging utilization for NVDA Blackwell and AMD MI450 — the most important forward-looking data point for the entire Semis cluster this week.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if TSM pulls back 5-7% from current levels toward $340-345 post-earnings, with SMA50 support confirmed on volume.

interconnected.name: Broadcom
interconnected.reason: Broadcom's custom ASIC business (Google TPU, Meta, Apple) is the primary competitor to NVDA/AMD in hyperscaler AI compute — AMD's Meta framework deal and GOOG's Intel IPU partnership directly affect how much custom silicon budget flows to AVGO versus merchant silicon.
interconnected.entry_note: Becomes interesting if AVGO pulls back 8-10% from recent levels following any hyperscaler capex guidance disappointment in Q1 earnings season.

interconnected.name: SK Hynix
interconnected.reason: The dominant HBM3E supplier to NVDA's Blackwell platform — MU's surge this week on helium supply/Hormuz reopening hopes makes SK Hynix the direct read-through for HBM pricing and memory market structure heading into the Q1 earnings cycle.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after MU's next earnings (no scheduled date in data) for memory pricing confirmation; SK Hynix KRW price divided by prevailing KRW/USD rate provides the USD equivalent for comparison.

active_risks: Iran-Hormuz Oil Crisis (Day 32): Strait of Hormuz effectively closed; maritime traffic halted; WTI near $98 with J.P. Morgan flagging $150/bbl tail risk if disruption persists to mid-May.
US-Iran ceasefire fragility: Ceasefire announced April 8 collapsed within 24 hours per Iran's claims; Trump issued Tuesday deadline for Iran to open Hormuz or face infrastructure strikes.
US-China tariff escalation: Section 122 surcharges (15%, 150 days) in effect after Supreme Court struck down IEEPA tariffs; China maintaining 34% retaliatory tariffs plus rare earth export controls.
MATCH Act regulatory risk: US lawmakers proposed tightening export controls on ASML's DUV equipment sales AND servicing to China — first major change since 2024.
geopolitical.market_impact: The Hormuz crisis is the dominant price-setter: WTI holding above $98 despite ceasefire attempts compresses margins for logistics-exposed names and keeps Singapore bank NIM headwinds structural via elevated US rate expectations. PLTR's 7.54% decline on the Burry-Anthropic thesis challenge and AMZN's 6.05% surge on Jassy's AI letter illustrate how individual catalysts are overriding macro sentiment — sector rotation within BigTech is accelerating.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Intel, Tesla, xAI, and SpaceX finalized the TeraFab manufacturing partnership (per Benzinga/Counterpoint Research April 10); Israel-Lebanon direct negotiation reports provided secondary market relief in the afternoon session (per Yahoo Finance); Trump issued formal Hormuz deadline for Iran on Thursday evening ahead of peace talks (per IBD).
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash reserves at cautious levels — VIX normalization to 19.49 is encouraging but the Tuesday Iran deadline creates a binary catalyst within 48 hours that argues against deploying new capital before the outcome is known.
